directions.name: Main-path explicit ldmatrix/mma.sync feed rewrite
directions.hypothesis: Round 14 showed the human epilogue vectorization is safe and near-neutral on the hot path, while MIO throttle stayed at roughly 41.7%. The next real lever is to rewrite the 64x128 main-path feed pipeline so the WMMA fragment-load sequence is replaced by an explicit shared-to-register ldmatrix / mma.sync flow, keeping the accepted round-14 epilogue behavior intact.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Main-path operand delivery and instruction mix before tensor issue, especially the WMMA fragment-load path feeding the 64x128 CTA kernel and showing persistent smsp__warp_issue_stalled_mio_throttle_per_warp_active pressure.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: TensorCoreTile128 tile/layout constants, especially the main-path B shared layout and warp-group shape
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: bf16_gemm_v1_tensor_core_kernel<TensorCoreTile128> K-loop around wmma::load_matrix_sync and wmma::mma_sync
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: existing vectorized epilogue block after accumulator writeback, which should be preserved unless a glue fix is strictly required
directions.risk: Highest implementation complexity of the three. Inline PTX register packing, lane mapping, and shared-layout assumptions can break correctness or inflate register pressure; the safe tail path and round-14 epilogue should remain stable to contain blast radius.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the rewrite fails correctness, materially raises register pressure / lowers occupancy, or leaves MIO throttle essentially unchanged after measurement, because that would mean the feed-path rewrite is not buying back tensor issue efficiency.

directions.name: Retile CTA and warp partition to trim per-warp N baggage
directions.hypothesis: The current 4x2 warp layout with a wide per-warp N fanout keeps the accepted main/tail split, but each warp still carries multiple N fragments and a relatively heavy B-side footprint. Retiling the CTA or warp work split to reduce per-warp N baggage should ease fragment handling and MIO pressure without abandoning the proven split between the 64x128 main path and the 64x96 tail path.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Per-warp fragment baggage and B-side staging pressure caused by the current CTA/warp partition, reflected in MIO throttle and possibly excess register footprint from carrying multiple N-side fragments per warp.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: kTensorWarpTilesM, kTensorWarpTilesN, and TensorCoreTileConfig warp/CTA shape constants
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: TensorCoreTile96 and TensorCoreTile128 specializations plus derived kWarpGroupCols and kTensorBlockN values
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: fixed-shape main/tail launch geometry in launch_bf16_gemm_v1
directions.risk: Medium risk. A narrower per-warp N assignment can reduce fragment baggage, but it may also weaken reuse, increase CTA count, or create a worse balance between tensor utilization and memory traffic if the new partition is too fine-grained.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the retile lowers per-warp baggage on paper but measured runtime regresses, tensor active drops, or DRAM pressure rises enough to offset any MIO improvement, because then the partition change is just moving the bottleneck.

directions.name: Direct-writeback epilogue that removes c_shared entirely
directions.hypothesis: Round 14 only vectorized the final stores; it did not remove the structural epilogue cost of writing accumulator fragments into c_shared and reloading them for global output. A direct-writeback path that bypasses c_shared entirely could reduce epilogue shared traffic and synchronization overhead, but it is likely a secondary lever unless the tensor feed path is already fixed.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Epilogue shared-memory staging and synchronization overhead after accumulation, specifically the c_shared scratch path and warp-level syncs around accumulator writeback.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: c_shared allocation and kCSharedTileElemsPerWarp configuration
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: warp_c_tile epilogue block using wmma::store_matrix_sync followed by float2 reloads and store_bfloat162_pair
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu: store_bfloat162_pair helper and any minimal kernel glue needed to support a direct global writeback mapping
directions.risk: Medium-high risk with uncertain upside. Mapping WMMA accumulator fragments directly to global BF16 stores is easy to get wrong, may require fragile lane-level unpacking, and could turn into a large rewrite for a bottleneck that looks smaller than the feed path.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if removing c_shared demands a brittle fragment-unpack scheme, cross-warp coordination, or only trims barrier/shared overhead without a meaningful runtime gain, because that would make it a worse next-step bet than fixing the main-path feed pipeline.

notes: This diagnosis incorporates the round-14 human-in-loop signal and avoids barrier/shared-B retry.
